name: Jarquez Hunter
headline: Buried behind Williams and Corum, strictly a handcuff dart
body:
Hunter enters 2026 fighting for the No. 3 job in a crowded Rams backfield, slotted behind workhorse Kyren Williams and Blake Corum, with Ronnie Rivers also in the mix for scraps. He didn't log a snap during the 2025 campaign, and his current consensus is unambiguous: a back near the very bottom of the position, going around 358th overall and rostered in well under one percent of leagues. That tells you everything about his standalone value right now.

Projections peg him at roughly 32 carries for 148 yards and a score, or about 22 fantasy points across a full season, which is bench-clogging territory in standard formats. The investment case is purely contingency. If Williams or Corum miss time, Los Angeles's run-heavy scheme could funnel real volume his way overnight. Until then, monitor camp reps and treat him as a late-summer stash, not a draft-day commitment.
